title: Bridge Essay: Effects of expectations and sensory unreliability on voice detection - A preregistered study.
concept: Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

**Effects of expectations and sensory unreliability on voice detection - A preregistered study.** — Conscious Cogn (2024). DOI: 10.1016/j.concog.2024.103718. PMID: 38880020. The phenomenon of "hearing voices" can be found not only in psychotic disorders, but also in the general population, with individuals across cultures reporting auditory perceptions of supernatural beings. In our preregistered study, we investigated a possible mechanism of such experiences, grounded in the predictive processing model of agency detection.
